sections.paragraph:
- Original: πλήρης

- Transliteration: Pleres

- Phonetic: play'-race

- Definition:

1. full, i.e. filled up (as opposed to empty)

a. of hollow vessels

b. of a surface, covered in every part

c. of the soul, thoroughly permeated with

2. full, i.e. complete

a. lacking nothing, perfect

- Origin: from G4130

- TDNT entry: 10:43,9

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From G4130; replete or covered over; by analogy complete: - full.
